fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Illinois CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Illinois - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity illinois, how to transfer CNA license to illinois, illinois CNA endorsement application

board.name: Illinois Dept of Public Health
board.mailing_address: Illinois Department of Public Health, Health Care Worker Registry, 525 West Jefferson Street, Springfield, Illinois 62761

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (based on employment and in-service education)

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Illinois.

steps.title: Enroll in an Approved CNA Training Program
steps.description: Enroll in and successfully complete a state-approved Nurse Aide Training Program. The Illinois Department of Public Health (IDPH) maintains a list of approved programs.

steps.title: Complete Required Training Hours
steps.description: Fulfill all program requirements, which include a minimum of 120 hours of training, with at least 40 hours dedicated to clinical experience.

steps.title: Pass the Nurse Aide Competency Exam
steps.description: Successfully pass the Illinois Nurse Aide Competency Exam within 24 months of completing your training program. This exam has both a written/oral portion and a skills demonstration portion.

steps.title: Program Submits Documentation to Registry
steps.description: Your approved training program will submit all necessary documentation regarding your successful completion and exam pass directly to the Illinois Health Care Worker Registry on your behalf.

steps.title: Verify Listing on Health Care Worker Registry
steps.description: Once your information has been processed by the IDPH, your certification will be active and listed on the Illinois Health Care Worker Registry. You can verify your status using the online search tool.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: Who administers the Illinois CNA exam?
faqs.answer: The Illinois nurse aide exam is administered by SIU-C (Southern Illinois University Carbondale) via nurseaidetesting.com.

faqs.question: How do I transfer my CNA to Illinois?
faqs.answer: Submit the Out-of-State CNA Application on nurseaidetesting.com with a $25 money order payable to SIU-C. A written competency retest is usually required.

faqs.question: How do I verify an Illinois CNA?
faqs.answer: Search the Illinois Health Care Worker Registry (HCWR) at hcwr.illinois.gov or call (844) 789-3676 (M-F 8:30am-5pm CT).

reciprocity.notes: Illinois reciprocity is handled through SIU-C Nurse Aide Testing. Submit the Out-of-State CNA Application with a $25 money order. A written competency retest is usually required.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in IL.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Illinois.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Illinois
hero.dek: To become a Certified Nursing Assistant in Illinois, you must complete an approved training program and pass the competency exam, after which your program will submit your information to the Illinois Health Care Worker Registry.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Illinois
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Illinois CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: For initial certification, individuals do not directly register on the Health Care Worker Registry. Instead, approved CNA training programs submit completion and competency exam pass information to the Registry on behalf of their students. The Registry is then used to verify an individual's certification status.
